metadata.task_title: Change the theme in Power Point Slide
metadata.instruction: Open Powerpoint Slide "Task ID 41.pptx" in Documents > Task ID 41 folder, and change the presentation theme to "Gallery".
metadata.context: I want to change my presentation's theme to a colorful one.

l3.rationale: Tabbing within the task switcher moves focus among windows.

l4.rationale: The user moves through system windows, opens File Explorer, browses to Documents > Task 41, and opens the PowerPoint file to reach the target presentation.

l5.rationale: This span covers navigating through the application and file dialogs to reach Documents > Task ID 41 and open Task ID 41.pptx, which is the file-launch phase of the task.

focused_element.name: Be careful—files from the Internet can contain viruses. Unless you need to edit, it's safer to stay in Protected View.

l3.rationale: F6 cycles focus within PowerPoint and moves toward the protected view controls.

l4.rationale: In Protected View, the user confirms the file should be editable by activating Enable Editing so changes can be made in PowerPoint.

l5.rationale: This span covers navigating through the application and file dialogs to reach Documents > Task ID 41 and open Task ID 41.pptx, which is the file-launch phase of the task.

l3.rationale: Tab moves focus to the Enable Editing button.

l4.rationale: In Protected View, the user confirms the file should be editable by activating Enable Editing so changes can be made in PowerPoint.

l3.rationale: Enter activates the Enable Editing button.

l3.rationale: Alt shifts keyboard focus to the ribbon and access keys.

l4.rationale: The user navigates to the Design tab, opens the Themes gallery, and selects Gallery to change the presentation's theme appearance.

l5.rationale: This final span covers the presentation formatting controls used to choose the theme and apply Gallery, matching the requested theme change.

l3.rationale: Right arrow moves among ribbon tabs toward Design.

l4.rationale: The user navigates to the Design tab, opens the Themes gallery, and selects Gallery to change the presentation's theme appearance.
